forces and newton's laws.prompt: A car is traveling at a constant velocity. What can be said about the resultant force acting on the car?
quizOptions: The resultant force is in the direction of motion
The resultant force is zero
The resultant force is opposite to the direction of motion
The resultant force is increasing

markScheme.point: The resultant force acting on the car is zero.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: When an object is traveling at a constant velocity, the resultant force acting on it is zero.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the condition under which force is directly proportional to acceleration
quizOptions: When mass is decreasing
When mass is constant
When mass is increasing
When velocity is constant

markScheme.point: For force to be directly proportional to acceleration, mass must be constant.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: For force to be directly proportional to acceleration, mass must be constant.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain the effect of air resistance on the motion of an object
quizOptions: Air resistance causes an object to accelerate
Air resistance has no effect on the motion of an object
Air resistance opposes motion through air
Air resistance increases the velocity of an object
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Air resistance opposes motion through air

markScheme.point: Air resistance is a drag that opposes motion through air.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: Air resistance is a drag that opposes motion through air.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Define the term 'resultant force' and explain its relationship to acceleration
quizOptions: Resultant force is the vector sum of forces and causes deceleration
Resultant force is the vector sum of forces and causes acceleration
Resultant force is the scalar sum of forces and causes deceleration
Resultant force is the scalar sum of forces and causes acceleration
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Resultant force is the vector sum of forces and causes acceleration

markScheme.point: The resultant force is the vector sum of forces acting on an object.
The resultant force causes acceleration in its direction.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The resultant force is the vector sum of forces acting on an object and it causes acceleration in its direction.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain how the force of friction affects the motion of an object
quizOptions: Friction acts between surfaces in contact and causes motion
Friction acts between surfaces in contact and opposes relative motion
Friction acts between surfaces not in contact and opposes relative motion
Friction acts between surfaces not in contact and causes motion
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Friction acts between surfaces in contact and opposes relative motion

markScheme.point: Friction acts between surfaces in contact.
Friction opposes relative motion or the tendency of relative motion.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: Friction acts between surfaces in contact and opposes relative motion or the tendency of relative motion.
